channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: GOP races to fund immigration enforcement. And, U.S. indicts former Cuban president
item.description: Republicans are racing to pass a $72 billion immigration enforcement package. And, the U.S. has indicted former Cuban President Raúl Castro for his alleged role in the 1996 downing of two planes.
item.content:encoded:
Republicans are racing to pass a $72 billion immigration enforcement package. And, the U.S. has indicted former Cuban President Raúl Castro for his alleged role in the 1996 downing of two planes.

(Image credit: Heather Diehl)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Researchers say the Trump administration is finding new ways to punish science
item.description: Even with federal grants largely restored, scientists say the Trump administration is still preventing those funds from reaching them. The consequences, they say, are already becoming clear.
item.content:encoded:
Even with federal grants largely restored, scientists say the Trump administration is still preventing those funds from reaching them. The consequences, they say, are already becoming clear.

(Image credit: Jodi Hilton for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: Amid allegations of abuse on Epstein's 'Zorro Ranch,' New Mexico opens new probes
item.description: Epstein owned a 10,000-acre property with a mansion. After calls by the public, the state attorney general searched the property and the state House created a "Truth Commission."
item.content:encoded:
Epstein owned a 10,000-acre property with a mansion. After calls by the public, the state attorney general searched the property and the state House created a "Truth Commission."

(Image credit: Roberto E. Rosales)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Montaño

item.title: Even as anxieties grow under Trump, these swing voters aren't ready to back Democrats
item.description: Swing voters in North Carolina say they are frustrated with President Trump and the state of the economy, but aren't ready to abandon him or his party as the midterms inch closer.
item.content:encoded:
Swing voters in North Carolina say they are frustrated with President Trump and the state of the economy, but aren't ready to abandon him or his party as the midterms inch closer.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez

item.title: COVID is shaping Americans' reaction to Ebola and hantavirus
item.description: Some Americans seem to be extra-alarmed about Ebola and hantavirus in the wake of COVID-19. But public health experts say they don't expect another pandemic this time.
item.content:encoded:
Some Americans seem to be extra-alarmed about Ebola and hantavirus in the wake of COVID-19. But public health experts say they don't expect another pandemic this time.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: A guide to converting your lawn into a wildlife friendly garden
item.description: Turning your grass into a garden isn't as complicated as you think, but it will take time and effort. This step-by-step guide breaks down the process, from killing your lawn to picking plants to grow.
item.content:encoded:
Turning your grass into a garden isn't as complicated as you think, but it will take time and effort. This step-by-step guide breaks down the process, from killing your lawn to picking plants to grow.

(Image credit: Celia Llopis-Jepsen/KCUR)
item.dc:creator: Celia Llopis-Jensen

item.title: Spencer Pratt is 'winning the internet,' but can he become mayor of Los Angeles?
item.description: Pratt, a former reality TV star, is flooding social media with edgy humor, AI slop and combative rhetoric as a way of grabbing attention and winning the vote of the very online. It's a strategy some political experts see as the future of online campaigning.
item.content:encoded:
Pratt, a former reality TV star, is flooding social media with edgy humor, AI slop and combative rhetoric as a way of grabbing attention and winning the vote of the very online. It's a strategy some political experts see as the future of online campaigning.

(Image credit: Andy Kropa/AP)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: The Education Department is hiring — while it's being dismantled
item.description: The Federal Student Aid office lost half its staff last year as part of Trump administration downsizing. Now, it's hiring hundreds of new workers.
item.content:encoded: The Federal Student Aid office lost half its staff last year as part of Trump administration downsizing. Now, it's hiring hundreds of new workers.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: As floods get worse, Britain tries a new solution: beavers
item.description: About 400 years ago, beavers were hunted to extinction across Britain. Now they're being reintroduced as little climate warriors, as communities harness their dam-building skills to mitigate flooding.
item.content:encoded:
About 400 years ago, beavers were hunted to extinction across Britain. Now they're being reintroduced as little climate warriors, as communities harness their dam-building skills to mitigate flooding.

(Image credit: Dan Kitwood)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: TSA's new 'Gold+' program looks to increase private security screening at airports
item.description: The agency calls the program an update to the Screening Partnership Program, in which 20 U.S. airports currently use private security screeners rather than federal workers.
item.content:encoded:
The agency calls the program an update to the Screening Partnership Program, in which 20 U.S. airports currently use private security screeners rather than federal workers.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell
